criteria.label: Compute H(X) from a discrete PMF by hand and in NumPy.
State and apply the 0 and log n bounds for entropy on n outcomes.
criteria.example: Explain that a fair coin has H = 1 bit (max for 2 outcomes) while p = [0.99, 0.01] gives H ≈ 0.08 bits, near the minimum 0.

criteria.label: Evaluate binary entropy H(p) for Bernoulli trials.
criteria.example: Compute H(0.9) = −0.9 log₂ 0.9 − 0.1 log₂ 0.1 ≈ 0.47 bits and compare to the 1-bit maximum at p = 0.5.

criteria.label: Compute information gain for a decision-tree split.
criteria.example: Parent [50, 50] has H = 1 bit; children [40, 0] and [0, 10] each have H = 0; weighted child H = 0; information gain = 1 bit.

criteria.label: Convert entropy between bits and nats correctly.
criteria.example: Given H = 2 bits, report H = 2 ln 2 ≈ 1.386 nats, not 2 nats.

criteria.label: Distinguish Shannon entropy from cross-entropy and explain when they coincide.
criteria.example: State H(P) = −Σ p log p vs H(P,Q) = −Σ p log q; they match when Q = P, e.g. both equal 1 bit for a fair coin modeled perfectly.
